72 year old. Male. with 18 years of education. The MMSE score is 29. The CDR score is 0.5. The logical memory score is 24. Subject is a rollover to ADNI3. He has been followed in ADNI since 2011. He was enrolled with a diagnosis of ""cognitively normal."" Prior to enrollment he was evaluated in a memory clinic for subjective memory loss and executive dysfunction, but was found to be cognitively normal on neuropsych testing. In 2014 his ADNI diagnosis was changed to amnestic mild cognitive impairment, a diagnosis was maintained at the 2016 visit. In reviewing the subject's neurocognitive test scores, his LM I and II have improved significantly since 2011. His Trails A and B scores are stable and not in the impaired range. Although his CDR global score has been 0.5 in the past three visits, including a 0.5 for memory, we believe this is consistent with the subject's long-standing subjective memory complaints. There is no objective test evidence that the subject has had memory or executive function decline since 2011. Rather, the evidence suggests a surprising improvement in logical memory (e.g., LM II score increased from 16 in 2011 to 24 in 2017). Therefore, we believe the subject should be classified as cognitively normal at this visit.. The diagnosis is CN.
